name: LEGO Technic Bugatti Chiron (42083)
description: Build an icon. The LEGO Technic Bugatti Chiron set faithfully recreates the legendary 1500 hp hypercar in stunning detail — from the distinctive horseshoe grille and distinctive blue-and-black two-tone finish to a functional W16 engine with moving pistons, working steering, and an 8-speed gearbox. A showpiece for collectors and a dream build for enthusiasts aged 16+.

specifications.value: 13 cm high × 56 cm long × 24 cm wide
W16 engine with moving pistons, 8-speed gearbox, working steering, openable doors, hood & rear spoiler

additionalInfo.value: LEGO Group, Aastvej 1, DK-7190 Billund, Denmark
3,599 LEGO pieces, numbered bags, illustrated build guide

reviews.title: The pinnacle of LEGO Technic
reviews.reviewText: Took me three weekends to complete and every single moment was enjoyable. The finished model is absolutely jaw-dropping — it sits on my desk and everyone who walks in the room immediately wants to know what it is. The gearbox mechanism is engineering genius.

reviews.title: Perfect gift for a car-loving adult
reviews.reviewText: Bought this for my partner who is a huge Bugatti fan. He spent three evenings building it and was smiling the entire time. The level of detail is extraordinary — the W16 engine alone is worth the price.

reviews.title: Best LEGO set I've ever built
reviews.reviewText: I've built over 40 Technic sets and this is the crown jewel. The complexity is satisfying rather than frustrating, and the end result is a centrepiece-quality model. Highly recommend to any serious LEGO fan.

reviews.title: Incredible model, steep price
reviews.reviewText: There is no question this deserves 5 stars for quality, engineering, and the building experience. I docked one star purely for the price point — it's a lot to spend. That said, for what you get it's probably justified.

reviews.title: A masterpiece of a toy
reviews.reviewText: My teenage son and I built this together over two weekends. It brought us closer and the finished car is stunning. The moving pistons in the W16 engine mesmerised us both. An unforgettable build.
